{{Quote|Oh my.|[[Obi-Wan Kenobi]], upon seeing a nos monster|<ref name="Death Utapau" />}}
'''Nos monsters''' were large [[Water|aquatic]] creatures that dwelled in the freshwater lakes, underground rivers, and sinkholes of the [[planet]] [[Utapau]]. 

==Biology and appearance==
The Nos monsters possessed a pair of large front [[Limb|fins]] that were used to paddle through the waters of their homes and stomp on any prey that wandered into the monsters' hunting grounds. Nos monsters also had large [[Color|green]] [[eye]]s that helped them see underwater and a tail that they swung as a weapon.<ref name="VE" />

{{Species-stub}}

==Behind the scenes==
[[File:Nosmonster.jpg|left|thumb|Digital model of the nos monster]]
The Nos monster was created for the [[2005]] film [[Star Wars: Episode III Revenge of the Sith|''Star Wars'': Episode III ''Revenge of the Sith'']] for the scenes on Utapau,<ref name="ArtofRotS">''[[The Art of Star Wars Episode III: Revenge of the Sith]]''</ref> although they didn't appear in the final film.<ref name="EpIII">{{Film|III}}</ref> Concept art of the "Grotto monster" was created by [[Iain McCaig]] and [[Michael Murnane]] and was presented to ''[[Star Wars]]'' creator and director [[George Lucas]] on [[April 11]], [[2003]], who approved of the design and requested for it's eyes to be made larger.<ref name="ArtofRotS" /> A digital model of the monster was then created.<ref name="Snapshot">{{SWArchive|url=episode-iii/explore/sithsnap/2006/05/sithsnap20060518.html|text=''Sith'' Snapshots: Sith Snapshots: Who's The Noss?}}</ref> The Nos monster made it's first appearance in the [[Star Wars Legends|Legends]] [[Star Wars: Revenge of the Sith (novelization)|novelization of ''Revenge of the Sith'']] by [[Matthew Stover]].<ref name="RotS novel">[[Star Wars: Revenge of the Sith (novelization)|''Star Wars: Revenge of the Sith'' novelization]]</ref> They later reappeared in the episode "[[A Death on Utapau]]"<ref name="Death Utapau" /> for the sixth season of the animated series ''[[Star Wars: The Clone Wars]]''. Although the show was canceled before the episode aired, it was released as unfinished story reels on [[StarWars.com]] in [[2014]] as part of [[The Clone Wars Legacy|''The Clone Wars'' Legacy]] and is still considered [[canon]].<ref name="YouTube">{{SWYouTube|vnH1JKr4o_8|Star Wars: The Clone Wars Legacy|sw_url=video/the-clone-wars-legacy}}</ref> The creature's model for the episodes was created by [[David Le Merrer]] and finished on [[March 26]], [[2012]].<ref name="Concept Gallery">{{SW|url=tv-shows/crystal-crisis-on-utapau-concept-art-gallery|text=Crystal Crisis on Utapau Concept Art Gallery|nolive=1}}</ref>

Concept art of the nos monster helped inspire the [[Shrikarai|Nameless]] for the ''[[Star Wars: The High Republic]]'' initiative.<ref name="Art of THR">''[[The Art of Star Wars: The High Republic: Volume II]]''</ref>
